title: Sesi Programming Language
description: A highly legible, buildable programming language designed for parsing text, orchestrating shell commands, and interacting with the file system. It features a tree-walking interpreter, static type safety with runtime flexibility, and native integration for external logic execution.
summary: Sesi is a TypeScript-based language that prioritizes simplicity and transparency. It provides a clean syntax for standard programming constructs (variables, functions, control flow) alongside specialized primitives for external service interaction, structured output, and memory management. The implementation is modular, consisting of a recursive descent parser and a tree-walking interpreter, supported by a comprehensive suite of built-in functions for I/O, networking, and concurrency.

code_examples.code: fn add(a: number, b: number) -> number {return a + b}

key_rules: The language uses a tree-walking interpreter for clarity and maintainability.
Expressions ending in '}' do not require explicit statement termination (semicolons or newlines).
